record.title: Palma Hanson Hove, 1975-06-13, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Mother was raised in Norway, father in Wisconsin. They arrived in the 1880's

point.title: Parents' coming to America; grandparents' homestead and shingle camp near Troy
point.synopsis: Parents' coming to America and Genesee. Grandparent's homestead and shingle camp near Troy. By wagon from Genesee to Troy.

point.title: Growing up on Cow Creek; ten children in a small house; move to Genesee
point.synopsis: Growing up on Cow Creek. Ten children in a small house - most families were large. Family's move from farm to Genesee; sons moved out of the area.

point.title: Girls expected to stay close to home; rarely did outdoor work
point.synopsis: Girls were expected to stay close to home. She rarely did outdoors work. Even when visiting, women were never idle with their hands.

point.title: Cooking for uncle's threshing crew; threshing crew; moving the cook wagon; harvest season
point.synopsis: Cooking for uncle's threshing crew - responsibilities and hard work. The threshing crew- migrants. Moving the cook wagon. Course of the harvest season.

point.title: Father's dislike of town boys; their farming
point.synopsis: Her father's dislike of town boys. Knowing husband for many years; their farming.

point.title: Two churches in early days of Cow Creek divided the community
point.synopsis: Two churches in the early days of Cow Creek divided the community.

point.title: Union of the churches; confirmation; church activities; Ladies Aid raising money
point.synopsis: More about union of the churches. Confirmation and other church activities for young people. Ladies Aid raised money for the church.

point.title: Circus parades; dancing despite church's disapproval; school; seasonal weather
point.synopsis: Children mistook circus parades for the circus itself. Dancing despite church's disapproval. School. Weather more seasonal in early days.

point.title: The Rosensteins; Native American intermarriage
point.synopsis: The Rosensteins, a Jewish family. Indian intermarriage.

point.title: Shivarees; father planted by signs; grandmother's stories
point.synopsis: Shivarees. Father planted by signs. Grandmother told stories about the "little folks."

point.title: 1893 wet harvest; father borrowing money
point.synopsis: 1893 wet harvest. Father borrowed money from Jake Kambitsch.

point.title: Getting by in the early days
point.synopsis: Getting by with a great deal of self-sufficiency in the early days.

point.title: Norwegian newspaper from South Dakota; men talked politics; importance of music; children's play
point.synopsis: Family got a Norwegian newspaper from South Dakota. Men talked politics, women didn't. Importance of music in her family. Children's play.

record.description: Cook wagon at harvesttime. Family farm life. Division of Cow Creek by two Lutheran churches. Young people's socializing. Early Genesee. 6-13-75 2 hr 48p
record.transcript: Transcript is available through the PDF download, too difficult to read typescript to display here.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
